repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X6 M50d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Forced regen or cleaning — worth it
Worth it on a vehicle worth £40,000+
Replacement often £5,500–£9,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW X6 M50d? Check These First
buyIf: * G06 (post-2019) B57S — the more reliable generation with front-mounted chain
* Full BMW service history with regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles)
* Documented timing chain history (F16) or 48V MHEV health check (G06)
avoidIf: * F16 pre-2015 with no timing chain history — the risk is real and expensive
* Any evidence of DPF or EGR issues — warning lights, reduced power
* Short-journey-only history — DPF issues are almost certain
* Poor service history — both engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The M50d is the pinnacle of BMW diesel engineering — triple-turbo (F16) and quad-turbo (G06) six-cylinder monsters with enormous torque. The F16 N57S is a powerhouse but has the rear-mounted chain risk; the G06 B57S is more reliable with front-mounted chain, quad-turbos, and 48V MHEV. Buy the G06 if you can, maintain the emissions systems, and the M50d will reward you with effortless performance.

cta.label: → Compare BMW X6 M50d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X6 M50d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Any rattle? N57 chain rattle is the biggest warning; B57S should be quiet
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — F16 (2014–2019) = N57S, G06 (2019+) = B57S
* Timing Chain History — N57: chains replaced? Tensioner checked? (pre-2015 is critical)
* DPF History — Ask about driving patterns; frequent short trips cause DPF issues
* EGR System — Any coolant loss or white smoke? EGR cooler failure is common
* Injector Performance — Check for smooth acceleration; injector issues are common on high-mileage cars
* 48V System (G06) — Any battery warnings? MHEV system checked?
* Turbo Response — Test acceleration; any hesitation or whistling? (complex turbo system)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying

items.question: How much does a BMW X6 M50d engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW X6 M50d engine costs £5,500–£9,000 supply only, varying by generation: N57S (F16) £5,000–£8,500, B57S (G06) £6,000–£10,500. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. These are specialist-only replacements — the multi-turbo systems require BMW diesel expertise.

items.question: Is the BMW X6 M50d reliable?
items.answer: That depends on which generation. The F16 N57S (2014–2019) has a rear-mounted timing chain with tensioner wear risk (pre-2015) — this is the defining issue. The G06 B57S (2019+) is significantly improved with front-mounted chain, but adds the 48V MHEV complexity. Regular maintenance and long journeys are essential. The G06 B57S is the more reliable choice.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW X6 M50d?
items.answer: N57S: timing chain tensioner wear (cold-start rattle, £1,500–£3,500), DPF/EGR issues (£400–£1,200), injector failure (£300–£800 per injector). B57S: DPF/EGR issues, 48V MHEV system issues (£800–£2,500), injector failure. Both: turbo actuator wear (£500–£1,500) due to complex multi-turbo systems.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW X6 M50d engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: X6 M50d values range from £15,000–£55,000 depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £5,500–£9,000 reconditioned engine represents 10–60% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained G06, replacement is almost always justified. For an F16 with other issues, assess scrap value first.

items.question: How do I find my BMW X6 M50d engine code?
items.answer: The M50d has used two engines: N57S (F16, 2014–2019, 381bhp, triple-turbo) and B57S (G06, 2019–present, 400bhp, quad-turbo with 48V MHEV). Check your VIN or registration year — pre-2019 is N57S, 2019+ is B57S. The code is stamped on the engine block.

items.question: Can I repair the N57 timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£5,000–£8,500). The job requires engine removal on the X6, making it a major investment. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic piston-valve contact.
